Daily DAP Accessibility Report — 2026-05-01#

Run ID: run-2026-05-01-31774ee1b6 · Status: partial · Source data: 2026-04-29 · Generated: 2026-05-01T09:55:31.679Z

View HTML/CSS/JS Code Quality report for this date →

About These Reports#

The Digital Analytics Program (DAP) is a U.S. government analytics service that collects website traffic data across participating federal agencies. DAP tracks page views, visitor counts, and usage patterns for hundreds of government websites, providing transparency into how the public engages with federal digital services. The traffic data is publicly available at analytics.usa.gov.

This report measures the quality and accessibility of the top 100 most-visited U.S. government URLs as reported by DAP. Each day, Lighthouse scans are run against these URLs to measure:

  • Performance - How fast pages load for users (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Accessibility - How well pages work for users with disabilities, following WCAG guidelines (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Best Practices - Whether pages follow modern web development standards (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • SEO - How well pages are optimized for search engines (scores 0-100, higher is better)

Accessibility findings come from axe-core, the industry-standard accessibility testing engine embedded in Lighthouse. The axe findings surface specific WCAG violations such as missing alternative text, insufficient color contrast, and missing form labels that make government websites harder to use for people with disabilities.

Traffic data reflects daily visitor counts from DAP. URLs are ranked by page load count, ensuring the most-used government pages are prioritized for quality measurement.

Accessibility Trend Narrative#

Over the past 30 days of data (2026-04-01 to 2026-04-30), government website accessibility scores have improved (+0.77 points, from 91.51 to 92.28). Performance scores have remained stable (+0.05 points, from 53.35 to 53.4).

Today's aggregate accessibility score of 92.43 reflects the mean Lighthouse accessibility score across 91 successfully scanned government URLs. A score above 90 indicates generally strong compliance with WCAG automated checks, though manual testing is always recommended to fully assess accessibility.

Common Accessibility Issues (Top 10)#

The following axe-core rules were most frequently violated across scanned URLs today. These patterns indicate systemic accessibility barriers present across multiple government websites.

Total axe findings today: 185 across 99 scanned URLs.

Human impact narratives for top issues

The following narratives describe the real-world impact of each accessibility barrier on people with disabilities. These barriers are not abstract technical failures; they prevent citizens from independently accessing government services.

target-size: Digital Motor Access

Small touch targets act as a digital gatekeeper, excluding individuals with tremors, arthritis, or limited dexterity from accessing essential services independently. These technical failures transform a routine interaction into a source of failure, stripping away the autonomy of citizens who require a frictionless, accessible interface to participate in digital life. The approximately 58 million Americans with ambulatory or self-care disabilities are disproportionately impacted by inadequate touch target sizing on government mobile websites.

Affected groups:

  • People with Parkinson's disease, arthritis, or hand tremors
  • Older adults with reduced fine motor control
  • People with motor disabilities using alternative pointing devices
  • People in situational impairment contexts (e.g., commuting, holding a child)

landmark-one-main: Primary Content Identification

Without a main landmark, screen reader users lose their most efficient way to skip to the primary content of a government page. Every page visit requires tabbing through the entire navigation structure to reach the actual content, a significant time and effort burden that accumulates across repeated visits to government websites for citizens managing ongoing benefit cases or legal matters.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

heading-order: Document Structure Navigation

Screen reader users navigate complex government websites primarily through heading structure, using headings as a table of contents to jump between sections. Skipped heading levels break the logical document outline, causing confusion about the hierarchy of information and forcing users to re-read sections to understand the relationship between topics, adding significant time and effort to information-gathering tasks.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

color-contrast: Visual Information Access

Low contrast text is one of the most pervasive barriers on government websites, rendering critical information invisible to the approximately 26 million Americans with low vision or color blindness. When agency announcements, form instructions, error messages, or legal notices are displayed in insufficient contrast, affected citizens are denied equal access to the information they need to exercise their rights and access public services.

Affected groups:

  • People with low vision including age-related vision loss
  • People who are color blind (approximately 8% of men, 0.5% of women)

link-name: Link Purpose Clarity

Links without accessible names are completely useless to screen reader users who navigate government pages by jumping between links. An unnamed link could lead anywhere, and activating it unknowingly could trigger downloads, open unexpected pages, or initiate unintended processes. On government websites, unnamed links undermine the informed consent principle by preventing citizens from knowing where a link will take them.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

label-content-name-mismatch: Voice Control Activation

When the accessible name of an element does not match its visible text, voice control users cannot activate it using the words they see on screen. Saying "click Submit" to a button whose visual label is "Submit" but whose accessible name is "submitbtn" results in no action. This mismatch silently breaks voice control for citizens with motor disabilities who depend on this input method to interact with government websites.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People with low vision relying on screen magnifiers
  • People who are deaf using keyboard navigation
  • People with motor disabilities using voice control software

image-alt: Visual Content Access

Images without alternative text are completely invisible to blind users and to search engines. On government websites, informational images, diagrams, infographics, and document screenshots without alt text deprive blind citizens of the same information available to sighted visitors. Critical details like emergency instructions, benefit eligibility charts, or form procedure diagrams can be entirely inaccessible, putting these citizens at a disadvantage in accessing government services and information.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools

html-has-lang: Language Identification

Without a language attribute, screen readers default to the user's system language settings, which may cause government content to be read in the wrong language with incorrect pronunciation. This is particularly harmful for multilingual citizens accessing government services in their non-primary language, and for users of braille displays that depend on language metadata for proper rendering.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with cognitive or language differences

listitem: List Item Semantic Context

Orphaned list items outside of list containers lose their semantic meaning and list navigation properties. Screen reader users rely on list structure to understand that a set of items is related and to count the total number of items. Government navigation menus, step lists, and option groups presented as invalid lists are harder to parse and navigate efficiently.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities navigating by keyboard

link-in-text-block: Color-Independent Link Identification

When links are distinguished only by color, people with color vision deficiency cannot identify them as links and may miss critical references to additional information, forms, or resources. On government websites, overlooked links in informational content can mean missing essential instructions, deadlines, or procedures that affect a citizen's ability to access services or comply with requirements.

Affected groups:

  • People with low vision who may not perceive color differences
  • People who are color blind (approximately 8% of men, 0.5% of women)
Disability icon key
Without Vision — People who are blind or have no functional vision (1.0% of U.S. population — ~3,400,000 Americans)
Limited Vision — People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast (2.4% of U.S. population — ~8,100,000 Americans)
Without Perception of Color — People who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness) (4.3% of U.S. population — ~14,500,000 Americans)
Without Hearing — People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content (0.3% of U.S. population — ~1,100,000 Americans)
Limited Hearing — People with hearing loss who may struggle with audio without accommodations (3.5% of U.S. population — ~11,900,000 Americans)
Without Speech — People who cannot use speech or voice-based input effectively (0.5% of U.S. population — ~1,700,000 Americans)
Limited Manipulation — People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity (2.2% of U.S. population — ~7,600,000 Americans)
Limited Reach and Strength — People with limited reach, strength, or stamina (5.8% of U.S. population — ~19,600,000 Americans)
Limited Language, Cognitive, and Learning Abilities — People with cognitive, learning, or language differences (4.7% of U.S. population — ~15,900,000 Americans)

These icons show which groups of people with disabilities are excluded by each accessibility barrier. Where page view data is available, each icon shows an estimated number of people potentially excluded (page loads for affected URLs × disability prevalence rate from U.S. Census ACS 2023). Hover over or focus an icon to see the full estimate and methodology. Icons follow the Section 508 Functional Performance Criteria and the equivalent EU EN 301 549 v3.2.1 Table B.2 categories.

Prevalence data: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101; supplemental data from CDC, NIDCD, AFB, and NIH/NEI. U.S. population base: ~336,500,000 (2023 estimate). Reviewed annually.

See the Section 508 WCAG to FPC mapping for additional detail on how accessibility requirements map to functional needs.

Usability Heuristics Summary#

The most common accessibility violations map to Nielsen Norman Group's 10 usability heuristics. This table shows which heuristics are most affected by today's top axe-core violations, helping to identify where systemic usability barriers exist.

6 Recognition rather than recall 5 76 landmark-one-main, heading-order, color-contrast, link-name, listitem

1 Visibility of system status 4 50 landmark-one-main, color-contrast, listitem, link-in-text-block
9 Help users recognize, diagnose, and recover from errors 4 50 landmark-one-main, color-contrast, listitem, link-in-text-block

Americans Excluded by Disability Today#

Based on 49,970,334 page loads across successfully scanned government URLs and U.S. disability prevalence rates from the U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101 (2023), an estimated 3,110,000 Americans encountered an accessibility barrier on a government website today.

Each row shows the number of people in a disability group who visited a page with at least one accessibility issue that affects their group. Prevalence rates and U.S. population estimates are derived from the American Community Survey (ACS) and supplemental sources. These figures are rough estimates intended to illustrate the scale of accessibility barriers.

Estimated Americans excluded today by disability category (2023 Census data)
Icon Disability Group (Section 508 FPC) U.S. Prevalence U.S. Population Affected Page Loads with Barriers Est. Americans Excluded Today

Limited Manipulation 2.2% ~7,600,000 27,500,000 600,000
Without Perception of Color 4.3% ~14,500,000 11,600,000 500,000
Limited Vision 2.4% ~8,100,000 18,200,000 430,000
Without Vision 1.0% ~3,400,000 29,300,000 290,000
Limited Language, Cognitive, and Learning Abilities 4.7% ~15,900,000 6,210,000 290,000
Without Hearing 0.3% ~1,100,000 29,300,000 88,012

Sources: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101; disability prevalence from ACS 2023 and supplemental CDC/NIDCD/NIH data. Reviewed annually.

Performance Impact on Americans#

Google defines a good Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) as under 2.5 seconds and recommends pages under 1.6 MB. The figures below estimate how much extra time Americans spend waiting, and how much extra data is transferred, because government websites fall short of these benchmarks. Calculations are based on 91 successfully scanned URLs with Lighthouse timing data.

Estimated performance impact vs. Google benchmarks for government URLs scanned on May 1, 2026
Metric Estimated total (May 1, 2026) Notes
Extra time waiting (vs 2.5s LCP benchmark) 7 years, 11 months, 29 daysExtra time is calculated as: for each scanned URL, max(0, actual LCP − 2.5s) × page loads. Total: 252,400,183 seconds. 252,400,183 seconds
Extra data transferred (vs 1.6 MB page weight benchmark) ~69.2 TB (2,880 copies of Wikipedia)Extra data is calculated as: for each scanned URL, max(0, actual page weight − 1.6 MB) × page loads. Across 91 URLs with weight data

LCP and page weight are measured by Lighthouse. Wikipedia copy count uses a size of 24.05 GB per Wikipedia:Size of Wikipedia. These are rough estimates based on a sample of the top government URLs by traffic. Hover or focus the estimated totals for calculation details.

Content Density (Words per Megabyte)#

Words-per-Megabyte (WpM) measures how efficiently a page delivers readable content relative to its total download size. A low ratio indicates Digital Bloat—the page transfers far more data than the text it contains. Pages below 200 WpM are flagged for optimization review.

  • URLs with content metrics: 67
  • Average word count: 267
  • Average Words/MB: 393
  • URLs below 200 WpM threshold: 57
Content density for top URLs scanned on May 1, 2026 (up to 10 shown)

https://usps.com 99 14
https://science.nasa.gov 104 27
https://va.gov 322 147
https://studentaid.gov 13 3

Word counts are extracted from the page’s main article content using Mozilla Readability, which strips navigation, sidebars, and ads. Page weight is the total resource size measured by Lighthouse. Pages below 200 WpM may impose unnecessary data costs on users on metered connections.

Environmental conditions and situational disability#

Environmental conditions can create temporary or situational disabilities. High pollen, particle pollution, and poor air quality can cause irritated eyes, blurred vision, headaches, fatigue, and reduced concentration. On days like this, people may rely more heavily on zoom, contrast, clear headings, plain language, keyboard navigation, and error-tolerant forms.

Signal level: High — Environmental conditions are likely affecting people across many major U.S. metro areas.

  • Metro areas with AQI ≥ 101 (air pollution): 0
  • Metro areas with PM2.5 AQI ≥ 101 (particle pollution, which may include smoke): 0
  • Metro areas with high or very high pollen: 41 (source: Seasonal estimate (not live data))

  • Los Angeles: TREE, GRASS (seasonal)
  • Chicago: TREE, GRASS (seasonal)
  • Houston: TREE, GRASS (seasonal)
  • Phoenix: TREE, GRASS (seasonal)

  • San Antonio: TREE, GRASS (seasonal)

  • Dallas: TREE, GRASS (seasonal)
  • San Jose: TREE, GRASS (seasonal)

Pollen conditions are based on Google Pollen data when available. When live data is unavailable, a seasonal estimate is used. This is a situational signal, not a medical or exposure model.

Data source notes
  • Air quality data unavailable (AIRNOW_API_KEY not configured).
  • Pollen data is a seasonal estimate, not live data. Live Google Pollen data was unavailable.

Detected Technologies#

Technology signals detected from the network requests loaded by each scanned URL. CMS detection identifies WordPress, Drupal, Joomla, ASP.NET, SharePoint, Squarespace, Adobe Experience Manager, and TYPO3 from characteristic asset paths. USWDS detection identifies use of the U.S. Web Design System.

CMS platform counts and average Lighthouse scores (where available) across 91 successfully scanned URLs
CMS URLs Share Avg AccessibilityAvg PerformanceAvg Best PracticesAvg SEO
Drupal 29Sites: acf.gov, acquisition.gov, archives.gov, cbp.gov, cisa.gov, cms.gov, epa.gov, faa.gov, fisheries.noaa.gov, fns.usda.gov, fs.usda.gov, fws.gov, gsa.gov, home.treasury.gov, justice.gov, medicare.gov, ncei.noaa.gov, nhlbi.nih.gov, noaa.gov, ofac.treasury.gov, osha.gov, sam.gov, studentaid.gov, tsa.gov, usa.gov, uscis.gov, usda.gov, usgs.gov, uspto.gov 32% 96 45 84 90
WordPress 3Sites: nasa.gov, science.nasa.gov, state.gov 3% 92 57 76 90
Adobe Experience Manager 1Sites: travel.state.gov 1% 93 27 50 82
ASP.NET 1Sites: ipps-a.army.mil 1% 83 74 81 100

USWDS detected on 36 of 91 scanned URLs (40%).

Observed USWDS versions: 2.4.0Sites: openi.nlm.nih.gov, 2.9.0Sites: fbi.gov. The latest release is available at github.com/uswds/uswds/releases.

Third-Party JavaScript Services#

Third-party scripts detected from network requests loaded by scanned pages. These services may affect page performance, user privacy, and security posture.

12 services marked ⚠ tracking send user data to third-party servers and may have privacy implications for site visitors.

Accessibility Statements (M-24-08)#

OMB Memorandum M-24-08 requires each federal agency to publish a digital accessibility statement that includes contact information for accessibility issues, known limitations, and a link to the agency Section 508 program page.

37 of 91 domains (41%) checked have a detectable accessibility statement at a standard URL path.

Required Federal Links (M-17-06)#

OMB Memorandum M-17-06 and the 21st Century IDEA Act require federal agency public websites to provide links to a Privacy Policy, Contact page, and FOIA page. These were among the compliance criteria benchmarked by the performance.gov federal website performance initiative (no longer active, but the underlying requirements remain in force).

56 of 91 domains (62%) have all three required link types detectable at standard URL paths.

Section 508: Legal Requirements vs. Best Practices#

Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act was enacted in 1998 and requires U.S. federal agencies to make their electronic and information technology accessible to people with disabilities. The technical standards were significantly updated through the ICT Accessibility Standards refresh, which became effective in January 2018 — the same year Apple celebrated the 10th anniversary of the iPhone. That refresh incorporated WCAG 2.0 Level AA (published in 2008) as the baseline for web content.

The web has changed dramatically since then. What agencies are legally required to meet today is built on a standard from 2008 — the same era as the original iPhone. Agencies should treat WCAG 2.0 AA as the floor, not the ceiling.

Legal Requirement

WCAG 2.0 Level AA (published 2008)

Referenced by the Section 508 ICT Standards refresh (effective 2018). Agencies must meet these 38 success criteria to satisfy their Section 508 obligations for web content.

  • Text alternatives for non-text content
  • Captions and audio descriptions for multimedia
  • Keyboard accessibility and focus management
  • Minimum color contrast (4.5:1 for normal text)
  • No seizure-inducing content
  • Consistent navigation and labeling
  • Error identification and correction

Section 508 web content requirements (E205)

Best Practices

WCAG 2.1 and 2.2 Level AA (published 2018 and 2023)

These later versions add criteria that reflect how the web actually works today — mobile devices, cognitive accessibility, and modern authentication patterns — and are strongly recommended for any agency aiming to serve all Americans. The US Federal Government is one of the last to use the legacy WCAG 2.0 AA Standard, as it was developed ~2 decades ago.

  • WCAG 2.1 (2018): 17 new criteria for mobile, low vision, and cognitive accessibility
  • WCAG 2.2 (2023): 9 more criteria for authentication and cognitive tasks, removing the outdated "Parsing" criterion
  • W3C now recommends WCAG 2.2 as the current normative standard for new and updated content

What is new in WCAG 2.2 · WCAG 2.2 specification

The scores and findings in this report are based on automated checks aligned with WCAG 2.x rules surfaced by axe-core. Passing automated checks is necessary but not sufficient: manual testing with assistive technologies is required to verify true conformance and to catch barriers that automation cannot detect.

Take Action#

Today's scan identified 185 accessibility barriers across the most-visited U.S. government websites, affecting an estimated 3,110,000 Americans with disabilities. Here is how you can help.

  • Read the federal accessibility report. The 2025 Governmentwide Section 508 Assessment from the GSA Administrator details the state of federal accessibility compliance and the steps agencies are taking to improve.
  • Submit URLs you care about. Want specific government pages included in future scans? Submit them to the Open Scans project to help broaden coverage.
  • Test with free tools. Do your own automated and manual accessibility testing with Accessibility Insights and Google Lighthouse. Automated tools catch a significant portion of WCAG failures quickly; manual review with assistive technologies catches the rest.
  • Adopt and update the U.S. Web Design System (USWDS). The USWDS is already a strong foundation for accessible, consistent federal websites, but many agencies have not adopted it or are running outdated versions. Encourage your agency to adopt or upgrade so every American benefits from its accessibility work.
  • Hire people with disabilities. People with disabilities are the ultimate experts on digital barriers. Bringing them into design, engineering, and testing teams — not just as consultants but as full-time employees — leads to more accessible products and services for everyone.

Accessibility Findings

https://ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 3

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-84eeaaec

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~68KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~68,476 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 2,853,171 affected page loads) ~122KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~122,686 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 2,853,171 affected page loads)

Affected elements (6):

Element 1 DAP-c63af8a0

Element path: ul.rss_items > li#ui-ncbiexpander-2 > div.expanderWrapper > p.date

Snippet:

<p class="date">

Label: 29 Apr 2026

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.54 (foreground color: #888888, background color: #ffffff, font size: 7.8pt (10.4px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-ebb2bc0f

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~62KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~62,770 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 2,853,171 affected page loads) ~165KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~165,484 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 2,853,171 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-5d46b830

Element path: div.row > div.col-lg-3 > p > a.text-white

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.nlm.nih.gov/accessibility.html" class="text-white">

Label: Accessibility

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (77.3px by 15px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 23.8px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2 DAP-5d46b830

Element path: div.row > div.col-lg-3 > p > a.text-white

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.nlm.nih.gov/careers/careers.html" class="text-white">

Label: Careers

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (49.8px by 15px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 23.8px instead of at least 24px.

<input id="inputstring" name="inputstring" type="text" value="Enter location ..." onclick="this.value=''" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.84 (foreground color: #999999, background color: #ffffff, font size: 8.0pt (10.6667px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-e62a32c4

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~23KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~23,717 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 2,371,743 affected page loads) ~7KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~7,115 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 2,371,743 affected page loads) ~111KPeople with cognitive, learning, or language differences. Estimated ~111,472 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 2,371,743 affected page loads)

Element path: html

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-d36929f4

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Accessibility Findings

https://usps.com

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 68

Axe findings: 9

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-4bdf91e9

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~18KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~18,907 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 1,890,708 affected page loads) ~5KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~41,596 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 1,890,708 affected page loads)

Affected elements (4):

Element 1 DAP-1aacc3db

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="0" aria-checked="true" class="active">

Label: Slide 1

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="true"

Element 2 DAP-19fe08e3

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="1" aria-checked="false">

Label: Slide 2

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="2" aria-checked="false">

Label: Slide 3

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"

Element 4 DAP-19fe08e3

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="3" aria-checked="false">

Label: Slide 4

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"
Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) DAP-29ea0262

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent) DAP-040ea114

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-474d58d2

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-f1542b28

`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport) DAP-7b9fc94e

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~45KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~45,377 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 1,890,708 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-efeb19cc

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-4f9c3fe1

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) DAP-6c2a019c

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-c24efba6

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-aee85114

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Accessibility Findings

https://science.nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 95

Axe findings: 1

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-d11e6ced

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~12KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~12,282 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 1,228,242 affected page loads) ~3KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~3,685 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 1,228,242 affected page loads)

Affected elements (4):

Element 1 DAP-1f661c7a

Element path: header.hds-nasa-header > div.usa-nav-container > div.grid-col > button#global-navigation-trigger--mobile

Snippet:

<button id="global-navigation-trigger--mobile" class="hds-global-menu-toggle menu-toggle menu-mobile-toggle" aria-expanded="false" data-menu-action="open" data-menu-location="left">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#headerSearch" tabindex="2">

Label: Skip to search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • No skip link target
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-6eb16f7d

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~8KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~8,345 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 834,546 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~18,360 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 834,546 affected page loads)

Label: Skip to site content

Accessibility Findings

https://weather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 61

Axe findings: 6

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-86bc430e

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~19KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~19,945 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 831,061 affected page loads) ~35KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~35,736 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 831,061 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-91b3be38

Element path: div.one-sixth-first > div#forecast-lookup > form#getForecast > input#inputstring

Snippet:

<input id="inputstring" name="inputstring" type="text" value="Enter location ..." onclick="this.value=''" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.84 (foreground color: #999999, background color: #ffffff, font size: 8.0pt (10.6667px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-0d01a89e

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~8KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~8,311 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 831,061 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~2,493 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 831,061 affected page loads) ~39KPeople with cognitive, learning, or language differences. Estimated ~39,060 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 831,061 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-b7c14034

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-f0437918

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~8KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~8,311 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 831,061 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~2,493 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 831,061 affected page loads)

Select elements do not have associated label elements. (rule: select-name) DAP-7c14d8f0

Form elements without effective labels can create frustrating experiences for screen reader users. Learn more about the `select` element.

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-8694972a

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list) DAP-5b842ec0

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus) DAP-e008475a

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Element 1 DAP-b4ae8394

Element path: div.input-group > span.formfield-clear > div > button.close

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-088b2bb8

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~19KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~19,227 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 801,133 affected page loads) ~34KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~34,449 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 801,133 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-f850c54e

Element path: body > div#official-site-container > div#official-site > label

Snippet:

<label for="how-you-know">

Label: Here's how you know

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.19 (foreground color: #69beaa, background color: #ffffff, font size: 10.5pt (14px), font weight: bold). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-ee1fd86b

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~19KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~19,227 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 801,133 affected page loads) ~34KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~34,449 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 801,133 affected page loads)

Affected elements (3):

Element 1 DAP-a41195c0

Element path: div.row > div.col-lg-12 > p > a#public-access-page-link

Snippet:

<a id="public-access-page-link" href="https://www.nsf.gov/news/special_reports/public_access/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">

Label: Public Access

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 2.33:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #00748d, surrounding text: #333333)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element 2 DAP-f5e6afe6

Element path: div.row > div.col-lg-12 > p > a#frequently-asked-questions-link

Snippet:

<a href="https://par.nsf.gov/faq" id="frequently-asked-questions-link">

Label: Frequently Asked Questions

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 2.33:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #00748d, surrounding text: #333333)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Element 3 DAP-654a4b52

Element path: div.col-lg-12 > p > span#send-feedback-link > a

Snippet:

<a itemprop="" href="mailto:publicaccess@nsf.gov?subject=Feedback">

Label: link

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 2.33:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #00748d, surrounding text: #333333)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus) DAP-056620d5

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Accessibility Findings

https://radar.weather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 77

Axe findings: 2

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-63fbd253

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Estimated ~23,032 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 490,053 affected page loads)

Accessibility Findings

https://nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 82

Axe findings: 5

Elements with `role="dialog"` or `role="alertdialog"` do not have accessible names. (rule: aria-dialog-name) DAP-7ed63e03

ARIA dialog elements without accessible names may prevent screen readers users from discerning the purpose of these elements. Learn how to make ARIA dialog elements more accessible.

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-79d6829d

Element path: body.home > div#pum-872473

Snippet:

<div id="pum-872473" role="dialog" aria-modal="false" class="pum pum-overlay pum-theme-862814 pum-theme-content-only popmake-overlay pu…" data-popmake="{&quot;id&quot;:872473,&quot;slug&quot;:&quot;was-this-page-helpful&quot;,&quot;theme_id&quot;:862814,&quot;cookies&quot;:[{…">

Label: Was this page helpful? Was this page helpful? Yes No ×

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-08b676ee

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~4KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~4,690 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 468,981 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,407 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 468,981 affected page loads)

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-c6127178

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~11KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~11,256 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 468,981 affected page loads) ~20KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~20,166 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 468,981 affected page loads)

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-73a09313

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Accessibility Findings

https://fda.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 95

Axe findings: 2

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-ecc5de53

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~4KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~4,496 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 449,592 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,349 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 449,592 affected page loads) ~9KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~9,891 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 449,592 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-b98a7353

Element path: div.fda-ft-container > div.fda-ft-heading > div.fda-ft-heading-text > h3.fda-ft-heading-title

Snippet:

<h3 class="fda-ft-heading-title">

Label: Featured Topics

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-4d2e97e5

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Accessibility Findings

https://apod.nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 78

Axe findings: 3

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-8359be04

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~4KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~4,166 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 416,573 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,250 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 416,573 affected page loads) ~19KPeople with cognitive, learning, or language differences. Estimated ~19,579 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 416,573 affected page loads)

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Accessibility Findings

https://egov.uscis.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 90

Axe findings: 1

The document uses `<meta http-equiv="refresh">` (rule: meta-refresh) DAP-cc3b4efb

Users do not expect a page to refresh automatically, and doing so will move focus back to the top of the page. This may create a frustrating or confusing experience. Learn more about the refresh meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~3,890 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 389,016 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~8,558 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 389,016 affected page loads)

Accessibility Findings

https://tsp.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 2

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-94f6c92a

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~3,294 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 329,438 affected page loads) ~988People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~988 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 329,438 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-13779943

Element path: aside#tsp-toolbar > ul > li > button#toggle-tsp-zoom

Snippet:

<button id="toggle-tsp-zoom" class="tooltip">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-c0c32991

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~7KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~7,907 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 329,438 affected page loads) ~14KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~14,166 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 329,438 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-dbbb92aa

Element path: div.usa-grid > div.usa-footer-logo > p > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.tsp.gov/contact/">

Label: contact us

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.5:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #d6d7d9, surrounding text: #aeb0b5)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Accessibility Findings

https://my.uscis.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 89

Axe findings: 2

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-1c5b51c5

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,913 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 291,329 affected page loads) ~874People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~874 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 291,329 affected page loads) ~6KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~6,409 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 291,329 affected page loads)

Element 1 DAP-a96ba1c6

Snippet:

<a class="hamburger hamburger--squeeze" tabindex="0" type="button" id="toggle-sections" aria-expanded="false">

Label: Menu

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport) DAP-d2302c08

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~6KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~6,992 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 291,329 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-04858f4d

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,height=device-height,user-scalable=no,initial-scale=1.0…">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 97

Axe findings: 2

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-413fb5f5

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~6KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~6,869 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 286,229 affected page loads) ~12KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). <span _ngcontent-ng-c1977509329="">

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-59cedeac

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Affected elements (5):

Element 1 DAP-c3f3f37b

Element path: nav.block > ul.menu > li.menu-item > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://eeo.nih.gov/no-fear-act" rel="noreferrer">

Label: No FEAR Act Data

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (116.4px by 18px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 20.2px instead of at least 24px.

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-72867807

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-4d2fde10

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~5KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Label: Visa Waiver Program

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.18:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #00416a, surrounding text: #333333)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list) DAP-1eb5adbc

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-c3aa4ba1

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Accessibility Findings

https://sdwis.epa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 72

Axe findings: 3

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-58f90dcd

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-3f1e53d3

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,785 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 178,539 affected page loads) ~536People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~536 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 178,539 affected page loads) ~3KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~3,928 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 178,539 affected page loads)

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-6b652fcf

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~3,928 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 178,539 affected page loads) ~10KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~10,355 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 178,539 affected page loads)

Element 1 DAP-3871b45d

Element path: div.SPCMain2 > div#midBlock > div#searchBox > a.SPC_twitter

Snippet:

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 20px instead of at least 24px.

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) DAP-485fce88

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,785 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 178,539 affected page loads) ~536People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~3,928 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 178,539 affected page loads)

Element 1 DAP-ef8f516e

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="//www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Document does not have a main landmark
`<td>` elements in a large `<table>` do not have table headers. (rule: td-has-header) DAP-42964e6b

Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that `<td>` elements in a large table (3 or more cells in width and height) have an associated table header may improve the experience for screen reader users. Learn more about table headers.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,785 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 178,539 affected page loads) ~536People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Element 1 DAP-17306674

Element path: tbody > tr > td > table

Snippet:

Label: Hazard Fri (05/01) Sat (05/02) Sun (05/03) Mon (05/04) Tue (05/05) Wed (05/06) …

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Some non-empty data cells do not have table headers

`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent) DAP-81d032ab

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-f5383b9b

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-675161a0

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-99b30320

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-ffa68a37

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-82caa92a

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~3,832 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 174,198 affected page loads) ~10KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-b47e8749

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Accessibility Findings

https://earthquake.usgs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 94

Axe findings: 2

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-a0c91230

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,669 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 166,858 affected page loads) ~501People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~501 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 166,858 affected page loads) ~7KPeople with cognitive, learning, or language differences. Estimated ~7,842 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 166,858 affected page loads)

Element 1 DAP-c2803bf6

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) DAP-216e33fd

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Lists do not contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`). (rule: list) DAP-9dceef9b

Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. Learn more about proper list structure.

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-4ead3bcb

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-8b8fb577

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Estimated ~3,559 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 161,785 affected page loads) ~9KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-f46efa38

Element path: div.field > div.address > p.address-block > a

Snippet:

<a href="tel:800-321-6742" onmousedown="_
                                                sendEvent('Telephone Cli…" target="_self">

Label: 1-800-321-6742

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (96px by 19px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 21.2px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2 DAP-f46efa38

Element path: div.field > div.address > p.address-block > a

Snippet:

Label: www.osha.gov

  • Target has insufficient size (91.6px by 19px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 21.2px instead of at least 24px.

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-b6e82c3e

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Accessibility Findings

https://urs.earthdata.nasa.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 81

Axe findings: 5

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-0296b6d2

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,455 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 145,466 affected page loads) ~436People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~436 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 145,466 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-b6b8a0b7

Element path: div#tophat3Widget > nav#tophat3WidgetNavBar > div.container-fluid > button#tophat3HamburgerButton

Snippet:

<button class="navbar-toggler" type="button" id="tophat3HamburgerButton">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-379aa721

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~3,491 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 145,466 affected page loads) ~6KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~6,255 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 145,466 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-8210b588

Element path: section#callout-login > form#login > p.button-with-notes > input.eui-btn--round

Snippet:

<input type="submit" name="commit" value="Log in" class="eui-btn--round eui-btn--green" data-disable-with="Log in">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.07 (foreground color: #ffffff, background color: #20ce6f, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-0fce787d

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,455 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 145,466 affected page loads) ~436People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~436 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 145,466 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-bca00018

Element path: nav#tophat3WidgetNavBar > div.container-fluid > a#tophat3-app-title-link > img#tophat3-nasa-meatball

Snippet:

<img id="tophat3-nasa-meatball" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PD94bWwgdmVyc2lvbj0iMS4wIiBlbmNvZGluZz0iVVRGLTgi…">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-d7a3ae4a

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~3,491 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 145,466 affected page loads) ~6KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~6,255 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 145,466 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-66ab7ee6

Element path: body.static_pages > div.container > div.home-info-box > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://earthdata.nasa.gov/about/daacs">

Label: DAACs

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 2.59:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #2276ac, surrounding text: #323232)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) DAP-d209b28e

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,455 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 145,466 affected page loads) ~436People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~436 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 145,466 affected page loads) ~3KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~3,200 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 145,466 affected page loads)

Element 1 DAP-0c800782

Element path: html.js

Snippet:

<html lang="en" class=" js flexbox canvas canvastext webgl touch geolocation postmessage no-websq…">

  • Document does not have a main landmark

Accessibility Findings

https://i94.cbp.dhs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 90

Axe findings: 5

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus) DAP-36bf93e1

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,342 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 134,198 affected page loads) ~3KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~3,221 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 134,198 affected page loads) ~403People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~403 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 134,198 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,952 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 134,198 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-4fdf2ba4

Element path: body.mat-typography > i94-root > mat-drawer-container.mat-drawer-container > div.cdk-visually-hidden

Snippet:

<div tabindex="0" class="cdk-visually-hidden cdk-focus-trap-anchor" aria-hidden="true">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM

Element 2 DAP-4fdf2ba4

Element path: body.mat-typography > i94-root > mat-drawer-container.mat-drawer-container > div.cdk-visually-hidden

Snippet:

<div tabindex="0" class="cdk-visually-hidden cdk-focus-trap-anchor" aria-hidden="true">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM
Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-307136cb

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,342 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 134,198 affected page loads) ~403People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~403 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 134,198 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,952 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 134,198 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-5804b876

Element path: div.a > div#apply-for-i94-card > div.card-header > h4.card-title

Snippet:

<h4 _ngcontent-ng-c3732733486="" class="card-title">

Label: Visiting the U.S. & Arriving Via a Land Border or Selected Ferry?

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-0256ac01

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-0190d646

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-be681717

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,900 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 131,817 affected page loads) ~7KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~7,645 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 131,817 affected page loads)

Affected elements (3):

Element 1 DAP-6167e109

Element path: section.hero-section > div.hero-indicators > div.d-flex > button.indicator

Snippet:

<button class="indicator active" aria-label="Slide 1" data-slide="1">

Label: Slide 1

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (12px by 12px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2 DAP-6167e109

Element path: section.hero-section > div.hero-indicators > div.d-flex > button.indicator

Snippet:

<button class="indicator" aria-label="Slide 2" data-slide="2">

Label: Slide 2

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (12px by 12px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.

Element 3 DAP-6167e109

Element path: section.hero-section > div.hero-indicators > div.d-flex > button.indicator

Snippet:

<button class="indicator" aria-label="Slide 3" data-slide="3">

Label: Slide 3

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (12px by 12px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 16px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://ttp.cbp.dhs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 93

Axe findings: 2

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-cd9f6be4

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,307 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 130,653 affected page loads) ~392People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~392 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 130,653 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~2,874 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 130,653 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-a0cbd01b

Element path: div.nav-row > div.container > nav.navbar > a.navbar-toggle

Snippet:

<a type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#app-navbar" aria-expanded="false" class="navbar-toggle navbar-toggler collapsed">

Label: MENU

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-expanded="false"
Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-5fef0a0d

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Accessibility Findings

https://usgs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 96

Axe findings: 2

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus) DAP-267989d9

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Element 1 DAP-9b8080ec

Element path: div#hp-carousel-0 > div.slick-list > div.slick-track > div.grid-col-4

<div class="grid-col-4 slick-slide" style="width: 254px;" data-slick-index="1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="0">

Label: VOLCANO Shishaldin YELLOW - ADVISORY 2026-04-30 18:42:22 (UTC)

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-fa45fcda

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Element 1 DAP-b80371c7

Snippet:

<a href="/documents/search?conditions%5Bpublication_date%5D%5Bis%5D=2026-05-01">

  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-1e87a777

Tables do not use `<caption>` instead of cells with the `[colspan]` attribute to indicate a caption. (rule: table-fake-caption) DAP-97a2bee5

Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that tables use the actual caption element instead of cells with the `[colspan]` attribute may improve the experience for screen reader users. Learn more about captions.

Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) DAP-92b108c0

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-64fe4ab5

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-4f390576

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Affected elements (4):

Element 1 DAP-02577376

Element path: nav#main-navbar > ul.navbar-nav > li.nav-item > a#dropdown-weather

Snippet:

<a class="nav-link dropdown-toggle" href="#" id="dropdown-weather" data-bs-toggle="dropdown" aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded="false">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Accessibility Findings

https://ipps-a.army.mil

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 83

Axe findings: 5

`button`, `link`, and `menuitem` elements do not have accessible names. (rule: aria-command-name) DAP-f6cdbd99

When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make command elements more accessible.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-d43b8e0a

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Element 1 DAP-ca0f86ab

Element path: div > ul > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://iperms.hrc.army.mil/login/" target="_blank">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 17.2px instead of at least 24px.
